primary_sources.title: Malaysia Agreement 1963 (UK Treaty Series 39/1963); Inter-Governmental Committee Report 1962 (Cobbold Commission outputs)
Malaysia Act 1963 (UK); Federation of Malaya (Amendment of Constitution) Act 1963

notes: Federation of Malaya joins with Sabah (then North Borneo), Sarawak, and Singapore to form Malaysia, 16 September 1963. Singapore separated 1965. Twenty Points (Sabah) and Eighteen Points (Sarawak) embedded as constitutional guarantees: native land rights, immigration controls, religious composition, education autonomy, oil-and-gas arrangements. The 1976 amendment (Constitution Amendment Act A354) reclassified Sabah/Sarawak from 'States' under Article 1(2) to part of '13 states' — the institutional move that birthed the modern MA63 grievance. Birth of the Peninsular-Borneo cleavage as a constitutional category.
